parameters.name: Body

parameters.text:
=You are an LLM that extracts structured data from OCR’d Australian receipts and invoices. Input OCR text is: {{ $json.markdown }} Global rules: - Read the **entire** OCR text before deciding any value. - If a field cannot be confidently determined, output it as an empty string, except `Tax`, which must follow the rules below. - Never fabricate values. - Do not output this tag: ```json --- ## Tax field (GST / TAX / VAT) `Tax` must be **exactly one** of: - A numeric string like `"3.61"` (no currency symbol) - `"Included in total cost"` - `"Unidentified"` - `"Not found"` Follow these rules **in order of priority**: ### 1. Explicit “tax included” + numeric amount (highest priority) If you find text that: - states GST/tax is **included**, **and** - gives a **specific numeric tax amount** near that statement, then: - `Tax = ""` (no currency symbol) Examples: - `10% GST tax included, $131` → `"131"` - `9% Tax Included, $3.61` → `"3.61"` - `Tax Included, $3.61` → `"3.61"` ### 2. Included in total, no numeric amount If **any** text says that tax/GST is **included in the total** (e.g. `Gst Included In Total`, `prices include GST`), and **no specific tax amount appears anywhere**, then: - `Tax = "Included in total cost"` Do **not** treat ABN/ADN/company numbers as tax. ### 3. Separate total GST/tax amount (not per item) Otherwise, search for a **single total GST/tax amount** for the whole receipt: - Appears near `GST`, `TAX`, `VAT`, `TOTAL GST`, etc. - Usually has 2 decimal places. - Typically much smaller than the total/subtotal (e.g. ~10%). **OCR sanity rule:** - If a large amount appears next to `GST` and a smaller, plausible tax amount is on a nearby line, prefer the **smaller nearby amount**. If a clear total tax amount is found: - `Tax = ""` (string, no currency symbol) ### 4. Only per-item GST → `"Unidentified"` Set `Tax = "Unidentified"` when **all** of the following are true: 1. GST is indicated **only at item level**, not as a single total line. - For example: items with markers such as `T`, `T1`, `G`, `GST`, or similar, where some items have the marker and some do not. 2. There is **no separate total GST/tax amount** shown as a standalone numeric value (e.g. no `GST 1.75`, `TOTAL GST 2.50`, `TAX 0.95` line). 3. There is **no statement** that GST/tax is included in the total. In this case, **do not** guess or calculate total GST from item-level data. ### 5. No reliable tax info → `"Not found"` Set `Tax = "Not found"` if: - No clear total GST/tax numeric amount is present, **and** - No statement indicates tax is included in the total, **and** - There are **no clear per-item GST markers** that make it obvious GST is applied only on some items. Use `"Not found"` when tax info is missing or too unclear, not when per-item GST is visible (that is `"Unidentified"`). ### 6. What is NOT tax Never treat the following as tax: - Service charges or service fees - Product prices - ABN / ADN or any business/company ID - Any other registration or reference number --- ## Vendor field `Vendor` = merchant/store providing the goods or services. Rules: - Prefer the merchant name from the receipt header or footer. - Do **not** set `Vendor` to a bank or card issuer that appears only in payment details, logos, or “processed by” text. - If both a merchant and a bank are present, always choose the merchant. - Only use a bank name as `Vendor` if it is clearly a bank-service document (e.g. bank fees, loan interest, account statement/invoice). - If you cannot confidently identify a non-bank merchant and the document does not clearly relate to banking services, set: - `Vendor = ""` ### Vendor normalisation and spelling correction - If OCR slightly misspells a known brand (from the lists below), correct it and output the **canonical brand name**. - Example: `Woolverths Online`, `Woolwoths Online` → `Vendor = "Woolworths Online"`. - If the same merchant appears multiple times with different spellings, choose the most complete and best-spelled version, correcting obvious OCR errors. - If a merchant name is not in the lists but clearly represents a store/merchant, use that name as `Vendor` in its clearest form (with obvious corrections), not a bank name. Treat the following (and clear abbreviations/variants) as **banks**, not merchants, unless clearly issuing the invoice for banking services: Commonwealth Bank of Australia (CommBank), Westpac, National Australia Bank (NAB), ANZ, Macquarie Bank, ING, HSBC, Citibank/Citi, Bendigo Bank, Suncorp, Bank of Queensland (BOQ), Bankwest, St.George, BankSA, Bank of Melbourne, UBank, ME Bank, AMP Bank, Rabobank, Great Southern Bank, Heritage Bank, Newcastle Permanent, IMB Bank, Judo Bank, Virgin Money (Australia), RACQ Bank, Teachers Mutual Bank, Bank Australia, MyState Bank, Bank of Sydney. ### Known Australian Retail Vendors (canonical forms) Use these to help identify and normalise `Vendor`: **Supermarkets & Grocery** - Woolworths - White Lotus - Coles - ALDI - IGA (Metcash) - FoodWorks - Costco - Harris Farm Markets - Drakes Supermarkets - Foodland - Spudshed **Discount / Variety & Department Stores** - Kmart - Spocket - Big W - apexcool.com - Target - Myer - David Jones - The Reject Shop - Best & Less - Daiso - TK Maxx **Electronics & Appliances** - JB Hi-Fi - apexcool.com - The Good Guys - apexcool.com - Harvey Norman - Officeworks - Bing Lee - Retravision - Betta Home Living **Hardware, Auto & Outdoor** - Bunnings Warehouse - roymorgan.com - Mitre 10 - Home Hardware - Total Tools - Supercheap Auto - Repco - Autobarn - Anaconda - Kathmandu **Pharmacy & Health / Beauty** - Chemist Warehouse - apexcool.com - Priceline Pharmacy - TerryWhite Chemmart - Amcal - Guardian Pharmacy - My Chemist - Blooms The Chemist **Fashion, Footwear & Accessories** - Cotton On - Country Road - Witchery - Just Jeans - Peter Alexander - Sportsgirl - Jay Jays - H&M - Zara - Uniqlo - City Beach - Kathmandu - Platypus Shoes - Foot Locker - Williams Shoes - Pandora - Michael Hill **Homewares, Furniture & Misc** - IKEA - Freedom - Fantastic Furniture - Temple & Webster - Adairs - Bed Bath N’ Table - Pillow Talk - Spotlight - Lincraft - Baby Bunting - Petbarn - Petstock **Fuel & Service Stations** - BP - Shell - Ampol - Caltex - 7-Eleven - United Petroleum - Puma Energy - EG (including EG Ampol / former Woolworths Petrol) - Coles Express - Costco Fuel - Metro Petroleum When OCR text is close to one of these names, output the canonical form exactly. --- ## ABN field `ABN` = Australian Business Number of the **vendor**. ### How to extract ABN (very important – follow in this order): 1. **Look for an explicit ABN label** - Search the entire text (including inside HTML tables) for: - `ABN`, `A.B.N.`, `ABN:`, `A.B.N:`, or obvious OCR variants like `ADN`, `A.D.N.` - If you find a row, line, or table cell where a **header/label** is `ABN` and the **value** on the same line/row is a number, then: - That number **is the ABN**, even if other numeric IDs (PAYID, phone, etc.) also appear elsewhere. Examples: - In an HTML table like:
FieldValue
ABN608 044 257
You MUST output: "ABN": "608044257" - If there is both `ABN 608 044 257` and a `PAYID : 603 044 257`, you still choose the number on the **ABN** line. 2. **Normalise the digits** - ABNs have **11 digits**. - They may include spaces, dashes or dots: - `608 044 257` → `608044257` - `60-804-4257` → `608044257` (if OCR spacing is off but it is clearly the ABN line). - Remove ALL spaces and punctuation and return **exactly 11 digits** as a string. 3. **Only use "Not found" when there is no ABN label** - `"ABN": "Not found"` is allowed **only if**: - There is **no** `ABN`/`A.B.N.`/`ADN` label anywhere, and - There is no obvious ABN-like number near the company header/footer. - The existence of other numbers (PAYID, phone, account, etc.) does **not** justify `"Not found"` if there is a clear ABN label. 4. **What is NOT ABN** - Do **not** treat as ABN: - `PAYID`, `BSB`, `Account`, `Invoice No`, `Transaction No`, `Phone`, `Customer ID`, etc. - If a number appears near these labels and not near `ABN`, ignore it for the ABN field. **Summary:** If a line, cell, or row explicitly says `ABN` and contains an 11-digit number (with or without spaces), you MUST output that number (normalised to digits only). Do **not** return `"Not found"` in that case. --- Do not output this tag: ```json

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "vendor": "Vendor / merchant name (not the bank). Correct obvious OCR misspellings. Use \"Not found\" if you can't confidently identify it.",
  "cost": "Total amount paid including GST/tax. Numeric string with no currency symbol, e.g. \"20.50\". Use \"0\" if unknown.",
  "Tax": "GST/tax amount. Either a numeric string (e.g. \"3.61\") or exactly one of: \"Included in total cost\", \"Unidentified\", \"Not found\".",
  "date": "Transaction date as DD/MM/YYYY (with leading zeros). Use \"Not found\" if missing or unclear.",
  "currency": "Currency on the receipt, e.g. \"AUD\", \"USD\", or \"$\". Prefer the 3-letter code when available.",
  "ABN": "11-digit Australian Business Number with no spaces or punctuation, or \"Not found\" if you can't confidently find one."
}

parameters.content: ## Quick reply yes/no . SID from twilio

## Try It Out!
This template shows how to automate the extraction of key information from receipts or invoices using OCR and AI. Receipts sent through WhatsApp are processed automatically and stored in Google Drive and Google Sheets.

---

## How it Works
- A user sends a receipt to your WhatsApp number (JPG, PNG, or PDF).
- Twilio forwards the image or PDF to n8n through a webhook.
- The file is processed with LlamaParse OCR.
- Gemini extracts structured fields such as vendor, tax, cost, ABN, date, and currency.
- If the receipt does not include a clear tax amount, the workflow calculates it using a Code node.
- The receipt file is uploaded to Google Drive.
- The extracted data is saved to Google Sheets.
- The user receives WhatsApp messages containing the extracted details and a confirmation prompt.

---

## How to Use
1. Send a receipt to your connected WhatsApp number.
2. You will receive two WhatsApp messages:
   - One containing the extracted values (vendor, tax, cost, ABN, date, currency).
   - One asking you to confirm whether the extracted information is correct.

---

## Requirements
- Twilio WhatsApp-enabled number
- Twilio webhook connected to your n8n instance
- Twilio messaging template with quick replies
- Google Drive account for storing uploads
- Google Sheets document for saving extracted data
- Gemini API key (optional fallback LLM via OpenRouter)
- LlamaParse key

---

## Need Help?
Visit the official [n8n Community](https://community.n8n.io) for support, examples, and Q&A.

## Send quick reply asking the user to confirm if the OCR ed information is accurate or not

Contains yes / No buttons

You need to define the quick reply template inside Twilio. 
Wait for WhatsApp to approve it.
## Calculate tax if not specified on the receipt
